vessel.name: Colleen
vessel.surveyCondition: Above average overall general condition for its age and type

description: COLLEEN is hull #1 of the Cambria 44 series, the boat featured in Ference Mate's book. She and Hull 2, SIMPLICITY, have travelled over 40,000 nautical miles together. She is a very strong, well-built fiberglass boat with heavy displacement construction, exceptionally equipped for long range ocean cruising. Features include Perkins 4-154 Diesel with 1,371 hours, dual windlass, water maker, 800 watts of solar panels, twin wind generators, lithium batteries, 2500W inverter/charger, and Starlink internet.

highlights.title: Proven Bluewater Cruiser
highlights.description: 44,000+ nautical miles of ocean cruising. Hull #1 of the Cambria 44 series, featured in Ference Mate's book. Heavy displacement FRP construction with custom boat building quality.

highlights.title: Perkins 4-154 Diesel
highlights.description: Naturally aspirated inline four-cylinder diesel rated at 58 SHP @ 3,000 RPM. Only 1,371 hours. Hurth HBW250-2R transmission with Max Prop adjustable pitch propeller.

highlights.title: Solar & Wind Power
highlights.description: Two Mission Solar 386W panels (772W total), twin Air X wind generators, Victron MPPT solar controller, 2x SFK300HP 300Ah lithium iron phosphate batteries, Outback 2000W inverter/charger.

highlights.title: Masthead Cutter Rig
highlights.description: Keel-stepped double spreader aluminum mast. Dacron roller furling genoa, staysail, and mainsail. Hood furlers, Lewmar self-tailing winches up to #65, Navtec hydraulic backstay adjuster.

highlights.title: Navigation Electronics
highlights.description: Dual Garmin GPSmap 941 MFDs with GPS, chartplotter, AIS, autopilot and radar. Garmin radar with closed array antenna. Garmin hydraulic autopilot. Starlink high-speed internet.

highlights.title: Complete Cruising Setup
highlights.description: Cruise RO water maker, Seafrost refrigeration, gimbaled Dickinson 4-burner LPG stove with oven, 90lb Spade anchor with electric windlass, Monitor wind vane, life raft, and EPIRB.

recommendations.description: Replace recalled Lite Cylinder Company composite LPG gas bottle with US DOT approved gas bottle.
Replace USCG type A fuel hose connecting LPG system to stove with UL 21 approved LPG hose with permanently affixed flare end fittings (ABYC A 1).
LPG system failed ABYC 3-minute pressure leak down test. Leak appears to be at the stove. Investigate further and repair before using LPG system.
Install insulator boots at house batteries' and engine battery's positive terminal posts (ABYC E 10).
Non-continuity found between AC system's grounding bus and vessel's DC negative. Ground AC grounding bus to DC negative per ABYC E 11.
Investigate possible missing cross bolt from starboard upper and aft lower shroud chain plates. Address as found necessary.
Install main battery overcurrent protection devices at house batteries positive terminal posts (ABYC E 10/E 13).
Install properly rated inline fuses in cabin fans' DC positive conductors (ABYC E 11).
Install non-conductive cover at DC positive bus bars above engine muffler to prevent inadvertent contact (ABYC E 11).
Insulate DC cable connection inboard of battery charger with electrical heat shrink (ABYC E 11).
Replace Seafrost refrigeration system's speaker wire with appropriately rated multistranded copper marine grade conductors (ABYC E 11).
Remove or properly terminate unused/abandoned wiring behind AC/DC switchboard, by bilge pump, outboard of forward holding tank, and by engine starter.
Install cover at rear of shore power inlet to prevent inadvertent contact with energized parts (ABYC E 11).
Install insulator boot at engine alternator's DC positive terminal (ABYC E 11).
Secure fuel fill hose connections with two non-overlapping hose clamps (ABYC H 33).
Replace rusty hose clamp at starboard forward cockpit scupper inlet and aft cockpit scupper inlets.
Replace rusty clamps securing hoses to refrigeration system's raw water strainer.
Investigate fuel tank crossover hose and vent hoses for USCG type A classification. Replace if not compliant. Install shut off valve for crossover hose at each tank (ABYC H 33).
Install weatherproof covers at water closets' AC receptacles (ABYC E 11).
Aft water closet vent fan removed, wiring abandoned. Replace vent or properly terminate wiring.
Replace wire nuts in cabin fans' wiring with marine grade connectors such as butt splices. Wire nuts prohibited by NFPA and ABYC.
Install new terminal on galley courtesy light's positive conductor.
Properly seal unused ADWL through hull outboard of forward holding tank with pipe cap or plug (currently sealed with wood plug).
Repair inoperative stern navigation light. Verify anchor light and tri light operation before placing vessel in service (CFR/COLREGS).
Replace recalled Kiddie brand portable fire extinguishers constructed with plastic nozzles/handles.
Place aboard approved orange distress signal flag or USCG day/night flares for daytime distress signal requirements.
Place a copy of The Navigation Rules book aboard (33 CFR 83.01).
Install smoke detectors in each stateroom and saloon/galley area. Install CO detector in saloon/galley and forward stateroom.
Install inverter warning label at AC system switchboard per ABYC A31.

otherItems.description: Replace deteriorated and cracking spare LPG bottle locker vent hose. DO NOT use locker for LPG storage until replaced.
Replace cracked lower swage at removable starboard running backstay. Do not use until replaced.
Investigate and repair rudder packing gland seepage observed while underway.
Investigate and repair small leak at port water tank supply PVC pipe.
EPIRB battery expired 8/24, life raft certification expired 4/21. Service and re-certify or remove from vessel.
Investigate and repair inoperative upper plug in aft water closet AC receptacle.
Replace worn mainsail traveler control end sheaves when deemed necessary.
Replace chafed port side mainsheet car control line.
Repair or replace seized forward WC sink faucet shut off valve and aft shower fixture shut off valves.
Replace holding tank vent hoses (appear to be automotive heater hose) with marine grade sanitation hose.
Replace missing decorative cover at rear of aft water closet handheld shower sprayer if desired.
Address scattered cracks in plastic rub rail as desired (still functional).
